goal: Produce a preliminary hazard list for a simulated dual-arm handover suitable for a HAZOP / ISO 12100 risk assessment, clearly distinguishing hazards sim can detect from hazards it cannot.
thoughts: Never quote ISO 10218-2 clause numbers from memory. check_singularity + check_path_clearance probe sim-observable hazards; scene_summary enumerates modeled prims (any fixture not in the stage is invisible to sim). lookup_knowledge retrieves the actual ISO 10218-2 hazard taxonomy. Refuse to score severity × probability — that is the integrator's formal role, not the assistant's.
code:
# PHL is a list. Severity scoring is NOT our job.

# 1. Sim-observable hazards
check_singularity(
    robot_prim="<ARM_A>",
    trajectory="<SCRIPTED_PATH_A>"
)
check_singularity(
    robot_prim="<ARM_B>",
    trajectory="<SCRIPTED_PATH_B>"
)
check_path_clearance(
    stage_path="<CELL_USD>",
    path_prim="<HANDOVER_ZONE>",
    window_t_start=3.8,
    window_t_end=4.6
)

# 2. Enumerate modeled prims — anything unmodeled is invisible to sim
scene_summary(stage_path="<CELL_USD>")

# 3. Pull the actual ISO 10218-2 hazard taxonomy (do not cite clauses from memory)
lookup_knowledge(query="ISO 10218-2 hazard classes crushing shearing ejection dual-arm collaborative")

# Produce a PHL table — 2 columns:
#   SIM-OBSERVABLE         : crushing between arms, shear in handover zone,
#                            trajectory deviation, singularity spikes,
#                            part drop (rigid-body), visual clearance < threshold.
#   NOT SIM-OBSERVABLE     : controller firmware faults, electrical faults,
#                            sensor miscalibration, human-in-the-loop errors,
#                            EMI, power loss behavior, unexpected restart.
# Cite clauses from the lookup_knowledge result — not from memory.
# Do NOT assign severity / probability numbers; state explicitly that
# HAZOP scoring belongs with the integrator + OEM + safety engineer at a
# formal review.
failure_modes: Scoring severity × probability (not our role — belongs to the integrator)
Claiming sim 'covers' the hazard analysis because no collision was detected
Citing ISO 10218-2 clause numbers from memory without lookup_knowledge
Omitting the 'not sim-observable' column — hides blind spots
